saints.en: St. Prophet Hosea. Ven. Protomartyr Andrew of Crete

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» - 6 стихир: 3 прор. і 3 прпмч., Сл.: прор., І нині: богородичний-догмат 3-го гласу. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прор., Сл.: прпмч., І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом тропаря прпмч. Відпуст великий з помин. цілого тижня, прор. та прпмч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар прор. (2), Сл.: прпмч., І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом тропаря прпмч. Катизми чергові, сідальні Октоїха. Канон Октоїха, прор. та прпмч. По 9-ій пісні – світильний Октоїха, прор., Сл.: світил. прпмч., І нині: богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прор., Сл.: прпмч., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прпмч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст великий з помин. цілого тижня, прор. та прпмч.
Часи: на 1-му – тропар і кондак дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му – прор. та 9-му – прпмч.
Літургія: вся Служба - дня. Ап. – 2 Кор. 174 зач.; 3, 12-18. Єв. – Лк. 22 зач.; 6, 1-10.

troparion_en.text: Trained in asceticism on the mountain, / with the weapon of the Cross you destroyed the spiritual assaults of the hostile powers, all-blessed one; / Again you bravely prepared for combat / and slew Copronymus with the sword of faith; / for both struggles you have been crowned by God, monk-martyr Andrew of eternal memory.

kontakion_en.text: Today the Queen of cities / celebrates the radiant feast of your light-bearing memory, / and invites every city and country to join her. / She rejoices for she has gained a great treasure: / your body which sustained many struggles, martyr Andrew, beacon of Orthodoxy.

troparion.text: We celebrate the memory / of Your prophet Hosea, O Lord; / through him we entreat You, / save our souls.

kontakion.text: (Podoben: “Today You have shown forth...”)Enlightened by the Spirit, your pure heart became the dwelling place of most splendid prophecy; / for you saw things far off as if they were near.  / Therefore, we honor you, blessed and glorious Prophet Hosea.

troparion.text: Trained in asceticism on the mountain, / with the weapon of the Cross you destroyed the spiritual assaults of the hostile powers, all-blessed one; / Again you bravely prepared for combat / and slew Copronymus with the sword of faith; / for both struggles you have been crowned by God, monk-martyr Andrew of eternal memory.

kontakion.text: Today the Queen of cities / celebrates the radiant feast of your light-bearing memory, / and invites every city and country to join her. / She rejoices for she has gained a great treasure: / your body which sustained many struggles, martyr Andrew, beacon of Orthodoxy.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Anthony, Abbot of Leokhnov, Novgorod

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Anthony, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyrs and Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian in Cilicia, and their brothers, Leontius, Anthimus, and Eutropius

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Translation of the relics of Saint Lazarus “of the Four Days in the Tomb”, Bishop of Kiteia, Cyprus

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Lazarus, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

oca_saints.name: “A Virgin Before and After Giving Birth” Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Let us sinners and wretched people now run most earnestly to the Theotokos,  /  and let us fall down in repentance, crying out from the depths of our souls: /   “O Sovereign Lady, have compassion on us,  /  help us, and come quickly, for we are perishing from a multitude of transgressions. /  Do not turn your servants away empty-handed, for we have you as our only hope.”

kontakion.text: We have no other help, we have no other hope, but you, O Sovereign Lady.  /  Help us, for in you do we hope, /  and of you do we boast, for we are your servants.  /  Let us not be put to shame.

oca_saints.name: “Deliverer” Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Like a bright star, your Holy Deliverer Icon shone forth with Divine miracles, /   illuminating those who are in the darkness of sorrows with the beams of your grace and mercy.  /  Therefore, All-Good Virgin,  /  grant us deliverance from troubles, /   the healing of spiritual and physical diseases, salvation, and great mercy.

kontakion.text: O Most Holy Lady, those who are in need turn to your Icon with faith, /  after being delivered from calamities by your protection;  / but as the Mother of Christ God, deliver us from cruel calamities, both temporal and eternal,  / that we may cry out to you: “Rejoice, our deliverer from every trouble.”

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Joseph, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

Your holy martyr Kozman, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Kozman;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Susanna, O Lord, / through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through her intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Susanna;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.
